# Use-cases — every consumer's published dependency

<!-- GENERATED by tools/catalog.py from the *.toml files — do not edit; run `make catalog`. -->

One row per `use-cases/*.toml`: a consumer declares **why** it needs a piece and **what** it relies on. This is the retention claim — a piece cannot be retired while an active row points at it (the Requester controls retirement; the Owner controls shape — the two hats).

| Consumer | Software | Status | Why necessary | What it needs |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| a2kay | anyembed | active | a2kay's semantic search over the vault needs embeddings without coupling to a specific model or serving stack. | The Embedder.encode() surface; the local BGE backend for offline indexing. |
| a2kay | anyllm | active | a2kay's agent and enrichment calls must not be wed to one LLM vendor; provider neutrality is a product requirement. | The neutral complete() surface + runtime backend selection (claude-code-cli locally, anthropic-api in prod). |
| a2web | anyllm | active | a2web's extraction/judge pipeline needs a metered, fail-loud LLM contract (tokens/cost/latency) across multiple backends, not a bare string. | The async Completion-returning complete() surface, fail-loud AnyLLMError (caught locally at Extractor/Judge to degrade to raw), and available()-gated backend selection. |
| a2kay | atomic-io | active | a2kay's frontmatter and ontology-store writes must be crash-safe -- a partial write must never corrupt a vault file a human or another process might read concurrently. | A tempfile+fsync+os.replace write so a crash or concurrent reader never sees a truncated file, and a failed write never destroys a pre-existing target. |
| a2web | browser-cookies | active | a2web can mirror the local browser's cookies so a fetch presents the user's logged-in session (the cookies_refresh tool). Reading each browser's on-disk cookie store — Chrome's Keychain-encrypted AES-GCM, Firefox's plaintext sqlite — is generic OS substrate. a2web keeps this optional/local-only (server containers have no browser); the package's optional [read] extra + lazy import match that: baseline-importable, degrades loudly when browser-cookie3 is absent. | read_cookies(source) -> list[CookieRow], the CookieRow / SameSite / CookieSource boundary types, and CookieAccessError / ChromeCookieAccessError for the degrade path — with browser-cookie3 as an optional, lazily-imported engine. |
| a2web | content-extract | active | For a normal article page, a2web wants the main content as markdown plus the structural facts a caller reasons over: title, byline, publish date, the heading outline, and the outbound links with a role (content vs nav vs boilerplate). This is generic page-to-structure substrate; which parts answer the question is a2web's policy. The body conversion is delegated to convert-md's convert_html so the trafilatura call is not hand-rolled twice. | extract_markdown(html, url) -> ExtractedContent (async) and parse_metadata(html) -> dict; ExtractedContent / ExtractedHeading / ExtractedLink boundary types; body markdown sourced from convert_html so it stays consistent with the shelf's conversion mechanism. |
| a2kay | convert-md | active | a2kay ingests heterogeneous documents (pdf/docx/pptx/html) into the vault as markdown; it must not own per-format conversion quirks. | The dispatch entrypoint + the model-free fidelity grade to gate low-quality conversions. |
| a2kay | duckdb-sidecar | active | a2kay's derived state (graph, audit, search, job history) lives in four separate per-concern DuckDB sidecar files, each opened the same way -- without a shared helper the connection dance drifts apart store by store. | One connect(path) that normalizes the target, creates the parent directory for a file target, and supports the :memory: passthrough, leaving schema/migrations/queries to each store. |
| a2kay | git-porcelain | active | a2kay's vault is git-backed; it needs safe, fail-loud git state queries without hand-rolling subprocess plumbing or risking credential drift. | Repo-state predicates (is_repo, dirty, conflict/merge checks) that preserve the user's git config. |
| a2web | html-fragment | active | Several a2web site handlers receive content as a pre-rendered HTML fragment inside a JSON API response — Discourse `cooked`, Habr `textHtml`, V2EX `content_rendered`, HN Algolia comment text, Reddit comment HTML. That fragment must become link-preserving markdown (or plain text) without a full-page extraction pass. The conversion is generic HTML substrate; which field carries the fragment is each handler's knowledge. | to_markdown(html, *, base_url) -> str and to_text(html) -> str; link-preserving, entity-decoded, relative hrefs absolutized against base_url. |
| a2web | http-cache | active | a2web caches fetched pages for conditional revalidation: ETag/Last-Modified round-trips, gzip-compressed bodies, TTL expiry, and a per-proxy-profile cache key so one URL caches differently per request profile. The cache mechanics are generic; the gate policy (a block page must never enter cache) stays in a2web. | CacheRow + free functions apply_schema / cache_get(conn, url, variant) / cache_put(conn, url, variant, *, ...) / conditional_headers(row), used against a2web's shared sqlite connection (so cache + extraction + cookie tables coexist in one file). |
| a2web | http-fetch | active | a2web's tiers (raw, archive) and every site handler perform HTTP GETs that must impersonate a browser TLS fingerprint, route through a proxy, ride a per-host circuit breaker, and never raise — mapping every outcome to a closed verdict. This was a2web's shared transport primitive; it is generic substrate, not the anti-bot moat. | One async fetch_bytes(url, *, proxy_url, cookies, conditional_extras, impersonate, breaker) -> FetchOutcome with a closed FetchVerdict; injected proxy + breaker so routing/stealth policy stays in a2web. |
| a2web | json-in-html | active | Many pages carry a machine-readable answer the prose does not: a Product/Recipe/ItemList LD-JSON block, microdata, OpenGraph tags, or a window.__NEXT_DATA__ blob. a2web mines that structured payload before falling back to prose extraction, and ranks candidates so the answer-bearing one wins. The extraction is generic HTML substrate; which payload answers the question is a2web's policy. | extract_json_payloads(html) -> list[JsonPayload], rank_payloads, is_answer_bearing, plus is_json_content_type / sniff_json_body / parse_json_response for raw-JSON responses. JsonPayload carries (source, data, script_id, byte_size). |
| a2kay | lean-wire | active | a2kay serves a knowledge base over MCP; agent-facing list/page results must be token-lean and line-oriented so a model reads rows, not quoted-newline noise. | encode_tsv (line-oriented TSV, cells never hold a raw tab/newline) + PruneEmpty/prune_dict/dump_model_for_wire for empty-field pruning on the wire. |
| a2kay | managed-region | active | a2kay's projection layer writes machine-owned sentinel blocks (HTML-comment-delimited) into otherwise human-edited files and must never disturb the human prose around them. | An idempotent replace-between-markers primitive, marker-agnostic, with a caller-supplied escape hook so untrusted/converted content can't smuggle in a corrupting second marker pair. |
| a2kay | mcp-result-wire | active | a2kay composes its MCP server directly on FastMCP (no a2kit App); it needs the generic result-formatting + list-projection middlewares that a2kit's server used to install, driven by a2kay's own per-tool plans. | FormatRoutingMiddleware(plans, consumer) to compress agent-facing content via page-tsv, and ListViewMiddleware(settings) for field-projection/pagination on list verbs. |
| a2kay | page-tsv | active | a2kay's MCP verbs return typed results (Page[T], lists, envelopes) that must render differently for an LLM caller than for a code/machine caller, from one owner so the two can't drift. | Page[T] + render(value, consumer, *, plan) (consumer is a parameter) + build_encoding_plan/infer_format_hint for type routing + the render_plain/encode_page_tsv_dict after-the-fact encoders for the result middleware. |
| a2web | record-mine | active | On a listing / search-results / forum-thread page, the answer is the repeated records (products, results, comments), which trafilatura discards as boilerplate. a2web recovers them: it mines the dominant repeated region and renders it to markdown so the caller sees the option space (feeding next_links / try_url). The detection + rendering is generic HTML substrate; which records answer the question is a2web's policy — and a2web never crowns a pick (ADR-0012). | extract_records(html, base_url) -> RecordSet | None; Record (text, links, heading_text, heading_link, depth, markdown) and RecordSet (records, container, child_signature, max_depth; is_threaded; to_markdown()). |
| a2web | settings-base | active | a2web configures from A2WEB_* env vars plus an optional YAML file, with ${ENV_VAR} references inside the YAML resolved at load and secrets (jina_key, tokens, signing keys) forced to env-only so they never live in a config file. That env/YAML plumbing is generic; a2web keeps its own AppSettings schema, its secret-field set, and its ~/.a2web/config.yaml path policy. | resolve_env_refs(value) for ${VAR} expansion, resolve_config_path(env_var, default) for the file-gated path, and SecretStrippingYamlSource(exclude=...) to drop the caller-named secret fields from the YAML layer. |
| a2web | sqlite-resource | active | a2web keeps its HTTP cache, its LLM-extraction answer cache, and its cookie mirror in ONE sqlite file behind a single lazily-opened connection — warmed on app lifespan, but never opened by a test that doesn't touch the DB. The connection lifecycle (lazy double-checked lock, WAL pragma, idempotent close, async-CM) is generic substrate, not a2web's fetching domain. | SqliteResource(db_path, *, pragmas, on_open) with ensure() / conn / close() / async-CM; schema applied caller-side via on_open so the primitive stays domain-free. |
| a2web | timefmt | active | a2web's response envelope reports timings (tier durations, total_ms) as human-readable strings, and its spec mandates ONE formatting site so durations render consistently everywhere. That adaptive ms→s→m:ss rule is generic substrate, not a2web's fetching domain. | fmt_dur(ms: int) -> str with the four-tier magnitude rule (integer ms under 1s, one-decimal s under 7s, integer s under 60s, m:ss above). |
